title: World Models for Robust and Adaptive Intelligent Control under Electromyographic Signals
description: Studying compact world models for robust and adaptive intelligent control under noisy, partial, and non-stationary electromyographic signals. The project uses an open myoelectric upper-limb prosthesis platform as a reproducible testbed to investigate latent representations, uncertainty estimation, model-based reinforcement learning, and multisession adaptation for EMG-based control.

title: Game-Theoretic Foundations for Robust Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
description: Developing robust multi-agent reinforcement learning methods grounded in game theory. The project studies how different game equilibria can improve robustness under diverse stress conditions, model uncertainty, and changing environmental dynamics.

title: Behavior Learning with Reinforcement Learning from Expert Demonstrations
description: Proposed, developed, and tested deep reinforcement learning algorithms combining expert demonstrations for controlling redundant robot manipulators, with research focused on biased exploration algorithms to guide the RL process through explicit exploration demonstrations.

title: Human Behavior Learning with Imitation Learning
description: Implemented and analyzed feed-forward neural networks for imitating human behavior in robot manipulator control, contributing to advancements in joint space learning and neural network applications in robotics.
